summary: No-ship: the root-cause flip and frozen RED/GREEN contract are grounded, but the destructive mutation seam still permits newly added human review state to be overwritten.

findings.title: Blocker: pre-push recheck lets the human-touch guard expire
findings.body: The initial decision suppresses rebuild when `humanTouch` exists, but after sibling reconciliation and ownership-query round trips the final query checks only `untrustedSameRepo`. If a reviewer adds a review, comment, assignee, or hold label after the initial scan, neither the ref OID nor PR ownership changes; this check passes and the following leased force-push rewrites the reviewed head, invalidating precisely the state H-4 promises to preserve. This is new evidence beyond accepted R-46: F-0 can reserve ref and PR creation, but cannot prevent human interaction with an existing trusted PR. F-1 would merely wrap and inherit this unsafe executor seam.
findings.recommendation: Make the final query an action-specific authorization revalidation. Before rebuild, require the same trusted PR and head, `humanTouch === null`, and no contested owner; abort if the target vanished or changed. Add a temporal witness where human touch appears only on the second query and assert zero push/create/arm.

next_steps: Add complete pre-push revalidation at the executor mutation boundary.
Freeze the temporal witness, then regenerate the RED/GREEN records without weakening existing assertions.
